audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Elihu continues: claim to speak for God, divine justice, discipline through affliction, warning against ransom/anger, and transition into divine greatness over rain, clouds, thunder, and light.

verses.restored: Elihu continued and said:
Bear with me a little, and I will show you, for there are still words for the Divine.
I will lift my knowledge from afar and give righteousness to my Maker.
For truly my words are not false; one complete in knowledge is with you.
Look, the Divine is mighty and does not reject; mighty in strength of heart.
He does not preserve the life of the wicked, but gives justice to the afflicted.
He does not withdraw his eyes from the righteous, but with kings on the throne he seats them forever, and they are exalted.
If they are bound in chains, caught in cords of affliction,
then he tells them their work and their transgressions, that they have acted proudly.
He opens their ear to instruction and says that they should turn back from harm.
If they listen and serve, they complete their days in good and their years in pleasantness.
But if they do not listen, they pass through the weapon and expire without knowledge.
The godless in heart store up anger; they do not cry for help when he binds them.
Their soul dies in youth, and their life among the shameful.
He rescues the afflicted through their affliction and opens their ear through pressure.
Indeed, he would have drawn you out from the mouth of distress to a broad place with no constraint, and what is set on your table would be full of fatness.
But you are full of the judgment of the wicked; judgment and justice take hold.
Beware lest wrath entice you into scoffing, and do not let a great ransom turn you aside.
Will your cry for help arrange you beyond distress, or all the forces of strength?
Do not long for the night, when peoples are taken up from their place.
Guard yourself; do not turn to harm, for you have chosen this rather than affliction.
Look, the Divine is exalted in his power; who is a teacher like him?
Who has appointed his way for him, or who has said, You have done wrong?
Remember to magnify his work, of which people have sung.
All humanity has seen it; a mortal beholds it from afar.
Look, the Divine is great, and we do not know him; the number of his years is beyond searching.
For he draws up drops of water; they distill rain from his mist,
which the clouds pour down; they drip upon humanity abundantly.
Can anyone understand the spreadings of the clouds, the thunderings of his pavilion?
Look, he spreads his light around him and covers the roots of the sea.
For by these he judges peoples; he gives food in abundance.
He covers his hands with lightning and commands it against the target.
His thunder declares concerning him; the livestock also, concerning what rises.
